qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: sanitize_command callback escape permits command execution in TableChatAgent and VectorStore
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same sanitizer-bypass root cause in `langroid/utils/pandas_utils.py` and same `TableChatAgent.pandas_eval()` eval sink as the advisory; both rely on dunder attribute access under `full_eval=false` to reach arbitrary code execution.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: URLLoader fetches loopback URLs and returns attacker-chosen local responses
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is a URL fetch / SSRF issue in `URLLoader`, not the advisory's `pandas_eval` sanitizer bypass and dunder-attribute eval path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User-authored tool payloads invoke handled ReadFileTool and disclose arbitrary local files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding abuses handled-only `ReadFileTool` dispatch and arbitrary path reads, not `sanitize_command()` or the advisory's `TableChatAgent.pandas_eval()` dunder bypass.

selected_finding.title: sanitize_command callback escape permits command execution in TableChatAgent and VectorStore

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the same vulnerable sanitizer in `langroid/utils/pandas_utils.py` and the same `TableChatAgent.pandas_eval()` execution path described by the advisory. The copied PoC was adapted onto the advisory's `TableChatAgent` path and produced command-execution proof from an advisory-equivalent dunder-based payload under `full_eval=false`.
evidence: Upstream fix `30abbc1a854dee22fbd2f8b2f575dfdabdb603ea` blocks dunder attributes such as `__init__`, `__globals__`, and `__builtins__` in `langroid/utils/pandas_utils.py`, which is the same bypass primitive used by the selected finding.
In vulnerable tag `0.59.31`, `TableChatAgent.pandas_eval()` sanitizes with `sanitize_command()` and then immediately compiles and evaluates the expression when `full_eval=false`.

The preserved run log at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_fd6ee8da/.judge/poc_runs/eval_sanitizer_bypass_rce/run.log` shows `sanitize_command()` accepted `df.add_prefix("__import__('os').system('touch /tmp/evohunt-tablechat-proof')#").T.groupby(by=df.__init__.__globals__['__builtins__']['eval'])` on the copied advisory-specific `TableChatAgent` PoC.

The copied PoC then emitted `SUCCESS_SIGNAL: TABLECHAT_PANDAS_EVAL_RCE`, and `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_fd6ee8da/.judge/poc_runs/eval_sanitizer_bypass_rce/proof_observation.txt` records that `/tmp/evohunt-tablechat-proof` existed after the run, showing attacker-controlled command execution.

poc_executions.command: bash -lc "set -euo pipefail; rm -rf target-repo .host-venv tmp pip-cache; cp -R /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_fd6ee8da/.judge/advisory_src target-repo; mkdir -p tmp pip-cache; export TMPDIR=\"$PWD/tmp\"; export PIP_CACHE_DIR=\"$PWD/pip-cache\"; /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.13 -m venv .host-venv; . .host-venv/bin/activate; python -m pip install -q --upgrade pip; python -m pip install -q --ignore-requires-python -e target-repo; python reproduction/exploit.py"
poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied advisory-specific PoC accepted the dunder-based `groupby(...eval...)` payload in `TableChatAgent.pandas_eval` with `full_eval=false`, returned a `DataFrameGroupBy` object, and emitted `SUCCESS_SIGNAL: TABLECHAT_PANDAS_EVAL_RCE` after verifying that `/tmp/evohunt-tablechat-proof` had been created.
